item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
The image that was used in this article has been replaced with an image that's cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) and is sourced as follows:

Title: scp1723-new.jpg
Author(s): N/A, Roget
Release year: 2020
Note: Created by Roget as an edited version of the image below

Source: pxhere
License: CC0
Title: N/A
Author: N/A

item.title: Operational Staff Post
item.content:encoded: As image is not CC compliant, it has since been removed.

item.title: Image provenance
item.content:encoded:
Tineye pointed to Getty Images, which says that this photo of an elderly Chinese woman was taken by Carl Mydans in 1941, for Life Magazine.

It is still under copyright, and Getty Images charges out the ass for its use.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In the last section:

we have interned her at a mental facility.

I never realized before now that "intern" is a synonym for "imprison".
Puts a whole new context on fetching coffee for no pay.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I find the idea of this poor lady getting tossed around between various GoIs very interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Was this ever going to be included in the article?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's not always a good thing to get anomalous abilities, regardless of how long it takes for it to catch up to you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ Show proposed edits
- Hide

To link to other gru content and to similar content regarding aged humanoids, I propose the following two crosslinks:

The subject is suffering from several degenerative conditions, including Alzheimer's disease, osteoarthritis, cataracts, and deafness in the left ear, with limited hearing in the right.

to

The subject is suffering from several degenerative conditions, including Alzheimer's disease, osteoarthritis, cataracts, and deafness in the left ear, with limited hearing in the right.1

and

It appears to have been used as the subject's diary during the time SCP-1723 was held captive by the GRU.

to

It appears to have been used as the subject's diary during the time SCP-1723 was held captive by the GRU.

Footnotes
1. Should the subject's condition continue to worsen, researchers are authorized to induce a medical coma in the subject according to the Palmer Protocol.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Please forgive my grammar mistakes. orz
I really really love this tragedy of a humanoid when I read it's translation in SCP-CN,I was so moved that I even tried to write a text about an idea comes to my mind after I read it.（Although I failed）This work is so great that I learned so many things about how to construct a humanbeing but not only a superman or something that not "humankind" .
Upvote , well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The subject is suffering from several degenerative conditions, including Alzheimer's disease, osteoarthritis, cataracts, and deafness in the left ear, with limited hearing in the right. Note that the deafness does not affect the subject's ability to "hear" radio broadcasts. In addition, SCP-1723 has suffered multiple broken bones and minor brain damage at some point in the past, which was not treated properly.

Jesus Christ this lady sure did have some real damage huh? Like jeez this is bad for a human being.

On the article though…I'm no-voting. Mostly because the whole radio intercepting thing does interest me, but doesn't somehow.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As much as I truly love this article, the thought still lingers in my mind, has anything significant happened to her since containment, how has she been, I'm truly curious. but maybe these are questions that truly don't need to be answered. Actually forget what I just typed up and accept my +1 for letting me have the privilege of reading this

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It feels great to know ones writing can affect someone this way. Thank you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I felt a sharp tug at my heartstrings when I read her log, describing her mental conditions and how her abilities made them worse over time; it was so tragic and sublime. It's a brief yet concise story and truly amazing. Good job. I had to +1 this one with all of my feelings.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think this is very, very nice. I love that it's not over the top or even that viscerally horrifying, yet still sad and thoroughly interesting. Also, it's not a white American male, which gets more kudos. +1'ed

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the compliment. I personally think this is my best work, despite how many people like Mr.Laugh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I find this exremely well done. It's rare in a human SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: She's different enough from a raccoon that this gets my upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Because it explicitly states that we recovered her from the Russians after a raid. :) According to the Word of God it was the CI, though I still prefer the thought of MC&D. Even though that would have to be British.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This started out just as interesting, but the idea of her being used as a spy to intercept enemy transmissions put it over. Well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Might want to touch that up then, or I could do it real quick if you want.

As said, other than that particular quibble, I love this one :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: anything originating within 300m was what I wanted to say.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I just have one little… Question, not sure if it's a complaint. She "is able to receive the contents of any radio waves passing within 300m of the subjects location", within the frequencies given. Does that mean any signal whose source of transmission is within 300m? Or where there's something actively receiving at those frequencies within 300m? Because otherwise, the way it's described breaks the laws of physics more than she breaks the laws of biology, as you actually have to have the radio wave pass through the antennae in order to receive it, and most broadcasting equipment beams out either more-or-less omnidirectionally or at least across an incredibly broad area. Even "tight" transmitters like the types used by covert intelligence agencies (which she seems to have been used to listen in on, and I like that angle) end up spreading out fairly wide at the end of the day.

All that said, it's still a great article, and I feel sorry for her not only because of her treatment and her overall predicament, but I can only imagine the sheer amount of noise she's had to deal with since this effect became prevalent, given the amount of radio-waves bouncing around the Earth at all sorts of frequencies, not to mention interference from solar flares, etc.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Who's to say it wasn't the Foundation that purchased her? Wouldn't be the first time…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very nice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes. Very good that you focused not on her captors, but on her. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I do like what you have done here in the end. nice thing to wake up to.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I agree about that last note. It's a good way to wrap up the tragedy.

Shouldn't this be sapient rather than sentient in the tags? Her mind is suffering, but it's still human.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The last addendum really tickles me, the idea that either MC&D or CI were trying to buy her off of the Russians, and that the Foundation intervened just in time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Liked the draft, love the article.

Good job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: thanks

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I upvoted this, which surprised me because I wasn't that impressed with the early drafts. This is the best kind of humanoid - it's about her predicament, not her powers. Nice work.
